reason: Item 07 covers public Optimizable wrappers (CurveLength, LpCurveCurvature, LpCurveCurvatureBarrier, LpCurveTorsion, ArclengthVariation, MeanSquaredCurvature, LinkingNumber, FramedCurveTwist) that were already JAX-native at the parent commit a9da18fac. The closeout test file pins two pre-existing coverage gaps (FramedCurveTwist f='lp' FD-Taylor parity, FramedCurveTwist {net,range,max} zero-derivative contract, LinkingNumber ncoils>=4 production-scale floor). No production hot path is modified.

cited_existing_production_tests: tests/integration/test_non_banana_example_cpp_jax_cpu_parity.py (length and curvature objectives in non-banana production fixtures)

tests/test_jax_import_smoke.py:1003-1024 (subprocess legacy curve objective value and gradient parametrizations covering CurveLength, LpCurveCurvature, LpCurveCurvatureBarrier, LpCurveTorsion, FramedCurveTwist)
production_scale_floor.nquadpoints_note: Scalar penalty kernels: nquadpoints from default create_equally_spaced_curves at order=6 is 90; FramedCurveTwist closeout fixture uses nquadpoints=32 to keep the FD-Taylor inner loop tractable.

dense_materialization_budget.framed_curve_twist_lp_fixture: per-curve (nquadpoints=32, 3) frame arrays (n1, n2, b1, b2, b1dash, n2dash) and (32,) twist profile
dense_materialization_budget.linking_number_ncoils_four_fixture: four (nquadpoints=90, 3) gamma arrays plus four matching gammadash arrays via create_equally_spaced_curves
dense_materialization_budget.framed_curve_twist_non_lp_contract: same as lp fixture; the dJ branch returns Derivative({}) without taking gradients

validation.red_step_command: git worktree add --detach /tmp/simsopt-jax-item07-red/wt a9da18fac; copy test file; JAX_ENABLE_X64=True JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu PYTHONPATH=src .conda/jax-0.9.2/bin/python -m pytest tests/geo/test_curveobjectives_item07_closeout.py -q
validation.red_step_result: 6 passed in 2.45s (expected; item 07 closes coverage gaps, not source bugs)
